segments.s749-g9: بماناد is an optative/wish-form verb (archaic/poetic third-person form, comparable to بماند) meaning “may ... remain.” In this clause the theme 'گیتی' (the world) is what is wished to remain, and the prepositional phrase به فرزند تو marks the beneficiary/possessor (literally “to/for your son”). Word order is slightly inverted for poetic effect: verb — subject — beneficiary.
segments.s749-g10: چنین هم is a connective adverbial phrase meaning “thus/so, likewise,” with هم adding the sense “also.” The verb is omitted here by ellipsis and is understood to be the same wish expressed in the previous clause (بماناد). به marks the beneficiary again; خویشان و پیوند تو together mean “your relatives and kindred/bonds,” i.e. other family/kin.
segments.s749-g11: چنین is an adverb meaning 'thus/so' placed before the verb for emphasis. داد is the past tense verb 'gave' (here 'gave a reply'); پاسخ is the direct object 'reply/answer'; بدیشان is the indirect-object clitic phrase 'to them'. The subject قباد (Qobad) comes at the end, a common poetic inversion (SOV order: object(s) + verb + subject).
segments.s749-g12: که introduces the content of Qobad's reply ('that' / a reported wish). همواره is an adverb 'always'. پیروز is an adjective 'victorious' that, with باشید (2nd-person plural subjunctive/imperative of بودن), yields a hortative/wish 'may you be victorious'. و is the coordinating conjunction 'and', and شاد is 'happy'. The clause is a blessing/wish in the optative/subjunctive mood.
segments.s749-g13: نباشیم is a first‑person plural negative hortative/subjunctive 'let us not be / let it not be'. تا here functions like 'that/so that' introducing the undesired result (i.e. 'that the ill‑doer be eternal'). جاودان is the adjective 'eternal', and بدکنش ('bad‑konash') is a compound noun 'wrong‑doer' or 'one of bad conduct' serving as the subject of the implied clause. Together the clause expresses a wish to prevent the wrong‑doer from enduring.
segments.s749-g14: چه is an exclamatory particle 'how'. نیکو is 'good/fine'. بود is the past copula 'was'. داد is used here as the noun 'justice' (not the verb). باخوش is an adverbial compound 'with gladness/cheerfulness' and منش is 'disposition/character', together functioning as an adjunct describing the manner of the justice. The clause is an exclamatory comment praising just conduct given cheerfully.
segments.s749-g15: جهان 'the world' is marked by the object/topic marker را, making it definite/direct. بداریم is first‑person plural present subjunctive/hortative of داشتن used here as 'let us hold/keep'. با ایمنی is an adverbial phrase 'with safety/securely' modifying بداریم. The whole segment is a hortative urging preservation of the world.
segments.s749-g16: ببریم is first‑person plural subjunctive/hortative of بردن 'to take/carry' used here as 'let us take away/remove'. کردار is 'deed/action' and آهرمنی is an adjective 'Ahrimanic' (of Ahriman, i.e. demonic), so کردار آهرمنی is the object 'the deeds/actions of Ahriman' to be removed. The clause pairs with the previous hortative to form a two‑part exhortation.
segments.s749-g1: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. شیروی is a proper name or epithet ('Shiruy' = the lion-like/heroic figure) as the subject. بنشست is a simple past (preterite) with the verbal prefix ب- often preserved in classical Persian; here it means 'sat down' or 'seated himself'. برتخت ناز is a prepositional phrase with بر + تخت ('on the throne') followed by ناز used as a noun meaning 'splendor/pride' — together 'on the throne of splendor'.
segments.s749-g2: به سر برنهاد is a light-verb construction: به سر + برنهاد = 'placed upon the head' (برنهاد = 'set/placed on'). آن کیی تاج آز is a noun phrase: آن 'that', کیی 'Kayanian/royal', تاج 'crown' and آز 'greed' specifying the kind of crown ('a crown of greed'). Word order is adjective/epithet after the head noun, typical of classical Persian poetry.
segments.s749-g3: برفتند is a plural past tense 'they went/advanced'. Gوینده ایرانیان is a nominative/agent phrase 'the speakers (orators) of the Iranians' (literally 'Iranian speakers'); classical Persian often allows the verb before the subject phrase for poetic rhythm, so 'they (the Iranian speakers) went forward.'
segments.s749-g4: برو here functions adverbially ('forward' / 'forth') modifying خواندند, the past plural 'they recited/sang/called'. آفرین is an exclamation 'bravo' or 'acclamation' and کیان is the object 'the Kians' (the royal house/kings) — together meaning they chanted praise for the Kians.
segments.s749-g5: همی‌گفت is an imperfective/habitual past form meaning 'kept saying' or 'would say' (common in epic narration). هریک 'each one' is the subject and به بانگ بلند is an adverbial phrase with به + بانگ ('with a cry/voice') and بلند 'loud', giving 'with a loud cry'.
segments.s749-g6: که‌ای here introduces the direct address 'that is, "O..."' (که + ای vocative fused in poetry). پر هنر is a compound adjective 'full of art/skill' (پر = 'full (of)', هنر = 'skill/virtue'). خسرو is a title/name (here used as 'Khosrow' / 'king') and ارجمند is an honorific adjective 'esteemed' or 'noble'. The phrase is a vocative: 'O skilled, esteemed Khosrow.'
segments.s749-g7: چنان هم که is a subordinating phrase 'just as / in the way that'. یزدان 'God' is the agent, تو را is the second-person object 'to you', and داد is the past 'gave'; تاج is the direct object 'crown'. The clause means 'as God gave you the crown.'
segments.s749-g8: نشستی is the second-person past 'you sat' (classical poetic past of نشستن). به آرام is an adverbial phrase 'calmly/at ease' (به + آرام giving manner), and بر تخت عاج is a locative 'upon the ivory throne' (بر + تخت 'on the throne', عاج 'ivory').
segments.s749-g25: بپردازم is the 1st-person singular present-subjunctive/jussive form of پرداختن with the verbal prefix بـ-, used here to express intention or future-directed action (“I will turn/attend to”). آن گه is a temporal phrase meaning “then” (آن = then; گه = time/occasion). به کار جهان is a prepositional phrase (به + noun) meaning “to/for the affairs of the world” where کار = “affair/deed” and جهان = “world/this life.” Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect: verb + temporal phrase + prepositional complement.
segments.s749-g26: بکوشم is the 1st-person singular present-subjunctive of کوشیدن (“I will strive/endeavor”). The following prepositional phrase به داد آشکار و نهان functions as its object: به = “for/to,” داد = “justice,” and آشکار و نهان are coordinated modifiers meaning “manifest/open” and “hidden/secret” (i.e. ‘justice both visible and concealed’).
segments.s749-g27: به جای is a compound prepositional expression meaning “in place of / in return for.” نکوکار is the beneficiary/target noun “the benefactor / virtuous person.” نیکی کنیم uses نیکی (good deed/goodness) plus کنیم (1st‑person plural subjunctive/imperative of کردن) meaning “let us do / we should do good.” The whole phrase means “let us do good in return for the benefactor.”
segments.s749-g28: دل مرد درویش is a noun phrase “the heart of the man/dervish” (دل = heart; مرد = man/person; درویش = dervish/poor person). رانشکنیم is a 1st‑person plural verbal form (jussive/subjunctive) meaning “let us break” used here metaphorically (“break the heart”). In context the line is a hortatory admonition: we should not break the poor dervish’s heart (the verb is used in the sense of breaking/hurting).
segments.s749-g29: دوتن is a collective subject meaning “both / two people.” بایدم (باید + ـم) is the modal “must / ought” agreeing with that subject. راد و نیکوسخن are two coordinated predicate adjectives: راد = “generous / noble,” نیکوسخن = “eloquent / of good speech.” The clause states an obligation: “both must be generous and eloquent.”
segments.s749-g30: کجا is an interrogative/adverb often rhetorical here meaning “how / where (would they)?” یاد دارند is a light verb construction where یاد = “memory/remembrance” and دارند (from داشتن) = “they have / they hold,” yielding “they remember.” ز کار کهن is a prepositional noun phrase: ز = poetic “of/from,” کار = “deed/affair,” کهن = “ancient/old.” The line asks rhetorically how they could recall the deeds of old.
segments.s749-g31: بدان is a fused prepositional/locative expression (به + آن) meaning “in that / to that.” انجمن گفت is the main clause “the assembly said” (انجمن = assembly; گفت = said/spoke). کاین is the fused relative/ demonstrative (که + این) introducing an embedded question; کارکیست is the fused sequence کار کیست = “who is the doer of the work/whose work is this?” — a poetic compacting of Persian word order (interrogative کی before the copula ست/کیست).
segments.s749-g32: ز here is the poetic preposition “from/of.” ایرانیان = “Iranians / countrymen.” پاک و بیدار are coordinated adjectives meaning “pure/virtuous” and “awake/alert.” کی ... ست is the interrogative clause “who is ...?” with کی = “who” and ست = copula “is.” The segment asks which Iranian is pure and alert — a continuation of the question begun in the previous segment.
segments.s749-g57: 'نباشد' is a negative copular/optative form meaning here 'was not' or 'let there not be'; 'همان' = 'that/same' points to the person just mentioned; 'نیز' = 'also/likewise' adds the sense of 'too'; 'هم‌داستان' = 'accomplice / like-minded (one)'. Together the phrase says that that same person was not likewise an accomplice.
segments.s749-g58: 'که' introduces a relative clause ('who/that'); 'پیشش' means 'in front of him / in his presence' (classical usage); 'کسی' = 'someone'; the verbphrase 'گوید این داستان' (poetic word order, verb before object) = 'tells this tale'. The whole clause restricts 'هم‌داستان' as 'one before whom someone tells this story.'
segments.s749-g59: 'دگر' = 'another' or 'on the other hand'; 'آنک' = relative 'that/who' linking to what follows; phrase 'گیتی پر از گنج تست' uses 'گیتی' (the world) + predicate 'پر از گنج' ('full of treasure') and the archaic/copular ending 'تست' (classical contraction equivalent to 'توست' = 'is yours'). Poetic inversion places predicate after the noun.
segments.s749-g60: 'رسیده' is a perfect/participial form 'has reached/arrived'; 'به هر کشوری' is a prepositional phrase 'to every country/land'; 'رنج' = 'suffering/hardship' is the thing that 'has reached', and again 'تست' functions as the copula/possessive 'is/yours' in archaic poetic usage. The clause means 'your hardship has reached every land.'
segments.s749-g61: 'نبودی' is past 'you were' / 'there was not' (past copula); here it reads with the prepositional phrase 'بدین' (= 'by this / with this') and 'نیز هم‌داستان' = 'also an accomplice'. Note that in the text 'بدین' is split into 'ب' + 'دین' but functions as the adverbial 'by/with this'. The clause denies that the addressee was an accomplice in this matter.
segments.s749-g62: 'پر از درد' = 'full of pain/sorrow' (پر + از + درد); 'کردی' is past 2nd-person 'you did / you made', used here as a causative: 'you caused to be full of pain'; 'دل راستان' = 'the hearts of the righteous' (دل = heart, راستان = the righteous). Poetic word order places the verb before its object.
segments.s749-g63: 'سدیگر' = 'another (one)' (an older compound meaning 'the other'); 'که' introduces the relative clause 'who'; 'چندان' = 'so/to such an extent' modifies the following adjectives; 'دلیر و سوار' = 'brave and a horseman' (two coordinated predicates describing that person).
segments.s749-g64: The relative clause continues: 'که بود' = 'who was'; 'اندر ایران' = 'in/throughout Iran' (classical 'اندر' = 'in'); 'همه نامدار' = 'renowned by all' or 'famous everywhere'. Word order is slightly inverted for poetic effect but reads as 'who was famed throughout Iran.'
segments.s749-g65: نبودند (past negative of بودن) + شادان (adjective) expresses a past-state: 'they were not glad.' ز here functions as a preposition 'about/with regard to' (poetic register), and فرزند خویش is a possessive phrase 'their child/son' (خویش = their own, built from خوی + ش). The whole segment means 'they were not glad about their son.'
segments.s749-g66: This continuation repeats the prepositional relation (ز) and lists objects of their sorrow: بوم و بر is a fixed pair meaning 'land and region/place,' پاک qualifies پیوند خویش ('their pure bond(s)'). گرامر: the initial ز still governs the whole coordinated phrase: '...nor regarding homeland and region and their pure kinship/bonds.'
segments.s749-g67: Elliptical nominative phrase: یکی ... یکی ... lists two destinations ('one ... another ...') with سوی meaning 'toward.' چین and روم are place names. The verb is omitted (a common poetic ellipsis): the sense is '(one went) to China, (another went) to Rome.' The word بد here is an archaic/poetic spelling likely standing for a past copula (بُد) or is a scribal/orthographic variant; it does not carry the literal sense 'bad' in this context.
segments.s749-g68: پراگنده گشته is a compound: پراگنده 'scattered' + گشته (perfect participle / passive/resultative of شدن) meaning 'has become scattered' or 'were scattered.' به هر مرز و بوم is a prepositional phrase 'to every border and land/region' (هر = every, مرز = border, بوم = homeland). Overall: 'they were scattered to every frontier and land.'
segments.s749-g69: دگر 'another/other' introduces a new person; آنک is a relative particle 'that/who,' linking to قیصر بجای تو کرد 'the Caesar made (him) in your stead.' بجای تو (بِ جایِ تو) means 'in your place,' and کرد is the past tense 'did/made/placed.' The clause literally: 'another whom the Caesar placed in your stead.'
segments.s749-g70: ز هر گونه 'from every kind/way' (i.e. in any respect), از تو 'from you,' چه تیمار خورد is a question phrase 'what care did he get/receive?' (تیمار = care/treatment). خورد here functions as 'befell/received' (not literal 'eat'), so the clause asks rhetorically what care came to him from you.
segments.s749-g71: سپه داد is a simple past clause 'he gave/sent an army' (سپه = army, داد = gave). The coordinated clause دختر تو را داد likewise uses را to mark the direct object 'your daughter' and نیز 'also' emphasizes the addition: 'and he also gave your daughter.' The subject is understood from context (the emperor/king).
segments.s749-g72: همان گنج 'that same treasure' (همان = the same/that), و با گنج بسیار چیز 'and with the treasure many things' — با introduces an accompanying set, بسیار modifies چیز as 'many things.' The sequence lists further gifts: 'that treasure, and along with that treasure many things.'
segments.s749-g121: گلینوش is the proper name (subject). گفت is the simple past of گفتن (‘said’) placed immediately after the name — a common narrative order in Persian. ای + جهاندیده مرد is a vocative: ای marks direct address and جهاندیده is an adjectival compound (‘world‑wise’), followed by مرد ‘man’; the vocative comes after the reporting verb.
segments.s749-g122: به کام تو is a prepositional phrase meaning ‘to/for your wish’ (به + کام). بادا is an optative particle meaning ‘may’ (a poetic/archaic wishive). همه کارکرد is a noun phrase ‘all (your) deeds/performances’ (همه modifying کارکرد). The clause is a conventional benediction: verb (may) + desiderative phrase + thing wished for.
segments.s749-g123: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause here (causal: ‘for/because’). تیمار بردی is a verb phrase: تیمار ‘care’ as object/noun + بردی (past of بردن) yielding ‘you took/paid care (of)’. ز is the poetic preposition ‘of/from’, and نازک تنم is the noun phrase ‘my delicate body’ with the possessive suffix -م on تن; word order is preposition + adjective + noun + possessive.
segments.s749-g124: کجا is a rhetorical interrogative here (literally ‘where’ but used idiomatically as ‘how/where would…?’). آهنین is a predicate adjective ‘iron/steely’. بود is the past tense copula ‘was’. پیراهنم ‘my tunic/shirt’ is the noun with possessive -م. The order places the question particle first, then predicate+copula+possessed noun to form the rhetorical question.
segments.s749-g125: برین is composed of بر + این (on/upon + this) meaning ‘upon this’ or ‘this very’. مهر is a noun ‘kindness/favour’. The second بر is the preposition ‘upon/with’ and آفرین functions as ‘praise/acclamation’ (an attributive/noun). خوانمت is the verb ‘I call/address you’ with the enclitic -ت marking the second‑person object; the clause literally means ‘I call you [by] this favour, [by] praise.’
segments.s749-g126: سزایی is a nominal idea of ‘desert/worthiness’ (‘a fitting reward’). که introduces a relative/explanatory clause (‘that/which’). گوهر is the object ‘gems, jewels’. برافشانمت is a compound verb: prefix بر‑ (on/upon/intensive) + افشان (root ‘scatter’) + the enclitic -مت marking the first‑person verb with second‑person object — here ‘I will scatter (gems) upon you’; the form has a future/intentive sense in poetic usage.
segments.s749-g127: نباشد is a negative optative/subjunctive form meaning ‘let there not be’ / ‘there should not be’. به جز functions together as ‘except’. خوب‌گفتار is a compound meaning ‘good‑speaking’ (eloquent/good words) and تو is the separate pronoun ‘your/you’ (possessive relation is expressed syntactically rather than by a suffix on خوب‌گفتار). The clause denies the existence of anything besides the addressee’s good speech.
segments.s749-g128: که again introduces a subsidiary clause (‘that’/‘so that’). خورشید is the noun ‘the sun’ (subject). بادا is the optative particle ‘may’ (a wish). نگهدار is a predicative noun ‘preserver/guardian’ and تو is the object/possessive ‘you/your’; the clause expresses a prayer: ‘may the sun be your preserver.’
segments.s749-g73: همی‌خواست is an archaic/poetic imperfective marker + verb meaning ‘intended/wanted’ (here: past/future intention). دار مسیحا is a genitive construction where دار (abode/house) precedes its modifier مسیحا (the Messiah) — ‘the house of the Messiah’. به روم is a prepositional phrase ‘to Rome’ (به = to; روم = Rome/Byzantium). The clause as a whole expresses an intention: ‘he intended [to bring/take] the Messiah’s house to Rome.’
segments.s749-g74: بدان is the older/poetic combination (ب- + دان) functioning here as ‘to that end / for that’ (purpose marker). تا introduces the purpose/result clause ‘so that / in order that’. شود is the present/passive/optative form of شدن ‘become’ used as the result verb. خرم‌آباد بوم is a doubled-place expression: خرم‌آباد (literally ‘pleasant/joyful abode’ or place-name) + بوم (dwelling/land) = ‘a pleasant dwelling/abode’. Together: ‘for that, so that it should become a pleasant abode.’
segments.s749-g75: This is a fronted-question/exclamation structure: به گنج تو = ‘to/for your treasure’ (به marks the beneficiary/target). از دار عیسی = ‘from the house of Jesus’ (از = from; دار عیسی = house of Jesus). چه سود = rhetorical ‘what profit/what use’. Word order is idiomatic Persian: beneficiary + source + question phrase.
segments.s749-g76: که here introduces an explanatory/casual clause (‘for/since’). قیصر is the subject ‘the Caesar’. به خوبی here means ‘in goodness/prosperity’ (به + noun: in/with). همی + شاد بود: همی is the continuous/poetic marker, شاد = ‘happy/rejoycing’, بود is past-tense auxiliary ‘was’. Together: ‘for the Caesar was rejoicing in prosperity.’
segments.s749-g77: ز is the poetic form of از ‘from’/‘because of’. بیچارگان is the plural noun ‘the wretched/unfortunate’. خواسته here functions as a noun ‘the plea/request’ (the petitions of the poor). بستدی is the past tense (archaic past) of بستن ‘to close’ — ‘you/he shut [them]’. The clause means ‘you shut off the pleas of the wretched.’
segments.s749-g78: ز نفرین = ‘from/because of a curse’ (ز = from/because of; نفرین = curse). به روی تو = ‘upon your face / upon you’ (به = on/toward; روی used here = upon). آمد بدی: آمد is past ‘came/occurred’ and بدی is ‘evil/harm’. The clause literally: ‘from a curse, harm came upon you.’
segments.s749-g79: ز یزدان‌شناس = ‘from/coming from the God‑knowing (pious) man’ (ز = from; یزدان‌شناس = one who knows God / a pious man). آنچ آمدت پیش = ‘that which came before you / that which was said to you earlier’ — آنچ = ‘that which’; آمدت (literally ‘came to you’) = ‘was brought to you/was said to you’; پیش = ‘before/earlier’. Whole: ‘what the pious man had told you before.’
segments.s749-g80: بر اندیش is an imperative: بر = ‘upon/on’ + اندیش (imperative of اندیشیدن) = ‘think upon / consider’. زان = ‘from that / about that’. زشت‌کردار خویش is a possessive phrase: زشت‌کردار = ‘bad/ugly deed (or evildoer)’, خویش = ‘one’s own’ (here split as خوی + ش: خوی = noun-root, ش = possessive clitic). Together: ‘Consider that wicked deed of your own.’
segments.s749-g137: This chunk names the giver of the counsel. لینِکن (لیکن) is an adversative particle 'but/now'; the personal phrase that follows identifies the agent: شاه ایران قباد = 'Qobad, king of Iran'. مرا (here written as م/را in the segmentation) is the recipient 'to me' placed before the subject (object-before-subject order is normal in Persian).
segments.s749-g138: بسی functions as an adverbial intensifier 'much/very/ample' modifying the following noun phrase. اندرین 'in these' + پند و اندرز 'counsel and admonition' is the direct object; داد is the simple past 'gave' (perfective) whose subject was named in the previous segment.
segments.s749-g139: که introduces a subordinate clause here ('that / so that / namely'). همداستانی (complicity, being of one mind) is used as the verbal noun and مکن is the negative imperative 'do not' (second person singular negative). روز و شب is a time adverbial 'day and night' intensifying the prohibition.
segments.s749-g140: A second that-clause: که + independent clause giving the practical consequence. کس = 'anyone/no one' as an indefinite subject; پیش here = 'before/in the presence of'; خسرو = 'the king'; گشاید is a third-person verb form 'opens' (optative/subjunctive flavor here: 'should open' / 'open'); دو لب literally 'two lips' = 'mouth' (i.e. speak). The structure forbids anyone from opening his mouth in the king's presence.
segments.s749-g141: مگر introduces an exception ('unless' / 'except'); آنک is a relative/conjunctive 'that/which'; گفتار او 'his speech' is the object of بشنوی 'you hear' — بشنوی here is second-person singular present/subjunctive ('you should hear / you hear'). The whole segment sets up the one permitted exception: to listen to what he says.
segments.s749-g142: اگر begins a conditional/alternating specification 'if / provided that'; پارسی گوید = 'he speaks Persian' (گوید here marks reported speech/speaking); ار پهلوی gives the alternative 'or Pahlavi' (ار functioning as an archaic/disjunctive particle). Together: 'if he speaks Persian or Pahlavi.'
segments.s749-g143: چنین گفت + name is the formula 'thus said [Name]'. اشتاد here is the speaker's name; کای is the vocative particle 'O' (often used poetically) attached to the addressee shadkam 'the fortunate one' — so this is the opening of direct speech addressed to 'O Shadkam.'
segments.s749-g144: من = 'I' (subject). اندر نهانی is an adverbial phrase 'in secret / secretly / any hidden matter'. ندارم is the first-person present negative 'I do not have' or 'I have not'; پیام = 'message' or 'tidings'. The clause denies possession of any hidden message.
segments.s749-g33: نمودند is a past tense plural verb 'they showed/displayed' placed before its objects as is common in poetic word order. گردان سراسر is a noun phrase 'the troop / whole host' with سراسر after گردان as an intensifier ('entire'). به + چشم forms a prepositional phrase 'before/in the sight of' that will take the following noun phrase (دو استاد) as its possessor ('the eyes of the two masters').
segments.s749-g34: دو استاد را is the object phrase 'the two masters' with را marking definiteness/emphasis. را‌گر here is actually را + گر: گر (an old/poetic conditional/subjunctive particle) introduces a purpose or 'lest' clause. نگیرند is present-subjunctive (third person plural) 'may not seize / do not seize' and takes خشم ('anger') as its logical subject; together گر نگیرند خشم = 'lest anger seize (them)'.
segments.s749-g35: بدانست is a past-tense verb 'he/it knew' appearing before its subject for poetic inversion. شیروی کایرانیان functions as the subject plus an attributive name phrase: شیروی (the brave/lion-like one) is the person who knows; کایرانیان ('the Kayanian Iranians') names the group concerned (here in apposition or as the domain of his knowledge).
segments.s749-g36: که introduces an indirect interrogative/relative clause 'whom/which'. را is the object marker attached to the pronoun-like element that follows (here marking the person to be chosen). برگزینند is third-person plural subjunctive/present 'they choose / should choose' used in an indirect clause. از میان is a fixed prepositional phrase 'from among', and پاک is an adjective 'pure' modifying the person to be chosen.
segments.s749-g37: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. The following words list personal names in series: اشتاد and خراد are names (here presented in apposition), with و as the coordinating 'and'. برزین is another name followed by پیر 'the elder' which modifies برزین; note that the epithet پیر follows the name as is normal in Persian.
segments.s749-g38: دو precedes adjectives/nouns to indicate 'two' (here 'two wise ones'), and the sequence دانا و گوینده و یادگیر enumerates qualities/roles: 'wise and speaker and learner'. The actual noun 'men' is omitted (ellipsis), so these words function as a descriptive apposition to the previously named persons.
segments.s749-g39: بدیشان چنین گفت is a common phrase 'he said to them thus' with بدیشان ('to them') preceding the verb گفت. کای is the vocative particle 'O' used to address someone, and بخردان is the vocative plural 'wise ones' (بخرد with a plural ending), so this segment introduces a direct address.
segments.s749-g40: This segment lists epithets in apposition to the vocative: جهاندیده is a compound adjective 'world‑seen' = 'worldly‑wise', کارکرده is a perfect participle 'having acted/experienced' = 'experienced', and ردان here functions as a final epithet or title attached to the addressees. The adjectives follow the noun (typical in Persian poetry) and together form the full form of address.
segments.s749-g217: نهان آشکارا بکرد این بهی is a compact, poetic clause where نهان and آشکارا are juxtaposed opposites ('hidden' / 'openly') and بکرد is the 3rd-person singular past of کردن used transitively ('made'). این بهی is the direct object ('this beauty/this excellent thing'). Word order places the verb before its object for emphasis, a common poetic structuring.
segments.s749-g218: که introduces a subordinate clause of result/condition ('that/so that'). بی تو ('without you') is a prepositional phrase (بی + pronoun تو). شود is the 3rd-person singular of شدن used here to mean 'become' (often with a potential/result sense in classical Persian). تخت شاهی is a compound 'throne of kingship' (royal throne), and تهی is the predicate adjective 'empty' — together ‘becomes empty’.
segments.s749-g219: سخن هرچ بشنیدی اکنون بگوی is an imperative instruction: سخن هرچ ('whatever speech/words') is the object phrase, بشنیدی is the 2nd-person past ('you heard') functioning as a restrictive relative clause modifying سخن هرچ, اکنون ('now') is an adverb placed before the main verb, and بگوی is an imperative/subjunctive form meaning 'say/tell' (classical poetic form).
segments.s749-g220: پیامش مرا کمتر از آب جوی continues the command: پیامش is پیام + enclitic -ش ('his message/his tidings'), مرا ('me' with object-marker split in the provided tokenization) marks the person to whom the tidings should be told, and کمتر از آب جوی is a comparative phrase 'less than the water of a stream' — here idiomatically meaning 'very slight / meagre'. The verb from the previous segment (بگوی) governs this object phrase.
segments.s749-g221: گشادند گویا زبان این دو مرد: گویا ('it seems / apparently') is a parenthetical adverb. گشادند is plural (3rd-person) and here means 'were wide/loose' and predicates زبان این دو مرد ('the tongues of these two men' — note the poetic word order). The plural verb agrees with the two tongues, and the phrase conveys that their speech was loose/expansive.
segments.s749-g222: برآورد پیچان یکی باد سرد: برآورد is a classical past 3rd-person singular of برآوردن meaning 'brought forth' or 'let out', پیچان ('twisting') modifies how it was brought forth, یکی ('one') refers to one of them, and باد سرد ('cold wind') is a metaphorical object (harsh words). The verb precedes the metaphorical object, a typical poetic inversion.
segments.s749-g105: به + جنگ here marks direction 'into/for battle' (چسبانده as a prepositional phrase). اندرون is an adverb/postpositional phrase 'within' (poetic placement after the preposition + noun), so به جنگ اندرون = 'into the battle/within the fight'. گرز پولاد is a noun + material phrase 'mace of steel' (word order noun + modifier). داشت is the past-tense verb 'he had / he carried' with the subject omitted (third-person singular implied).
segments.s749-g106: همه دل = 'the whole/entire heart' (همه before noun = entire). پر از is the idiom 'full of' (پر + از + noun). آتش و باد are two nouns joined by و; here آتش conveys inner passion/ardor and باد an image of impetuous force (literary 'wind'). The final داشت is the same past verb 'had/was filled' (copular/possessive sense).
segments.s749-g107: چو is the conjunction 'when'. What follows are two proper names (خراد برزین is a name compound; اشتاگشسپ is an irregular/corrupt proper name in the manuscript). There is no verb in this segment because it is a temporal subordinate introduced by چو and the verb appears in the next segment.
segments.s749-g108: فرود آمدند is the compound verb فرود آمدن in simple past, 3rd person plural: 'they descended/they alighted'. The subject appears after the verb (inverted/poetic order): این دو دانا = 'these two, wise ones' (demonstrative + numeral + adjective used as noun). از اسپ is a prepositional phrase 'from the steed/horse' (از = 'from').
segments.s749-g109: گلینوش is the feminine proper name. بر پای جست is an idiomatic verb phrase: بر = 'upon', پای = 'foot', جست (past of جهیدن) = 'leapt/sprung' → 'sprang to her feet'. آن زمان is an adverbial phrase 'at that moment/then' placed after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s749-g110: ز here functions as 'because of / from' (poetic z) introducing the cause: 'because of the sight/meeting'. دیدار = 'seeing/meeting', ایشان = 'them' (referring back to the two), ببد is an older past form meaning 'became' (not simple 'was'), and شادمان = 'happy/joyful'. The whole means 'she became joyful on seeing them.'
segments.s749-g111: بجایی (بجا + ی) = 'in the appropriate place' (adverbial/locative). که introduces a relative clause 'where/that'. بایست is a modal/old-form meaning 'ought to / should' (not literal 'stand' here). بنشاندشان is the verb بنشاندن (to seat, causative 'make sit') in past/inflected form with the object clitic -شان 'them' — 'seat them' / 'set them down'.
segments.s749-g112: همی is a discourse adverb (here an immediacy/emphatic particle 'then/just'), مهتر = 'the chief/elder', نامور = 'renowned/famed' (attributive), and خواندشان is the past 'he called them' with the object clitic -شان. Word order places the adverb first, then the title, the epithet, and the verb.
segments.s749-g169: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when' introducing a temporal clause. دیدند is simple past 'they saw'. بردند here is a past transitive verb 'they took/led/went (and did something)' and forms part of the idiom نماز بردن پیشِ کسی — 'to offer/pray before someone'. پیشش is پیش + clitic ش (before him/in his presence). نماز is the noun 'prayer/devotional act' and is the direct object of the idiom.
segments.s749-g170: ببودند is an archaic past form related to بودند (they were / they remained) — here 'they stayed/they were'. هر دو functions as 'both' (هر modifying دو). زمانی دراز is a time-expression: زمانی 'a time/for a while' and دراز 'long', together 'for a long time'.
segments.s749-g171: جهاندار is the subject 'the sovereign' (a title). بر is the preposition 'on/upon' indicating the surface or target of an inscription. شادورد here is a proper name or epithet (appearing after the preposition); بزرگ is a postpositive adjective 'great' modifying شادورد — standard Persian adjective-after-noun order in poetry.
segments.s749-g172: نوشته is an impersonal participial/nominal use 'it is written' / 'had written' (introducing the content of the inscription). همه پیکرش = همه + پیکر + ـش: 'all his body' where ـش is the third-person possessive clitic. میش و گرگ are nouns 'ewe (sheep) and wolf' giving the literal content of the inscription; the phrase functions as the quoted/reported text of نوشته.
segments.s749-g173: همان is the demonstrative 'that/same'. زر و گوهر is a coordinated noun phrase 'gold and jewels'. بافته is a past participle 'woven' describing how the metal and gems appear. برو here is a poetic/archaic locative element attached to بافته (literally 'woven on it' — likely a contraction of بر + او or similar); its exact modern equivalent is unusual, so it functions to mark that the weaving is on the surface.
segments.s749-g174: سراسر is an adverb 'throughout' / 'all over'. یک اندر دگر is a formulaic phrase 'one into another' meaning mutual interweaving; اندر is the preposition 'in/into' and دگر 'other/another'. تافته is the adjectival/past participle form of 'weave' ('woven'), so the whole phrase says the thing is woven throughout with elements interlaced.
segments.s749-g175: نهالیش is نهال + ـش: نهال 'sapling/young person' with the third-person possessive clitic ـش 'his' — thus 'his young (one)'. در زیر is the fixed prepositional phrase 'under/beneath' and دیبای زرد is the noun phrase 'yellow brocade' with زرد postpositive adjective 'yellow'. The segment describes the young person being under the yellow brocade.
segments.s749-g176: پس پشت او is a locative phrase: پس (here 'after/at the rear') + پشت 'back/behind' + او 'his' = 'behind him' or 'at his back'. مسند لاژورد is a noun phrase 'a seat/throne (مسند) of lazhward (lapis-lazuli)' with لاژورد as a material/color specification. Word order places the material/name after the noun, a common poetic arrangement.
segments.s749-g209: بخواهد + شدن is an older/fixed future/subjunctive-like construction: بخواهد (will/desire) governs شدن (to become/happen), so together here mean 'it will come to pass / should happen that ... becomes'. بخت is the subject (fortune), and زین is actually 'زِ این' ('from/of this') attached to دودمان ('dynasty/house'); the whole phrase = 'if fortune is to depart from this dynasty'.
segments.s749-g210: نماند is a negative/negative-resultive verb meaning 'will not remain / will be left no more'. درین is در + ین = 'in this', تخمه is 'offspring/seed', کس is used in the sense 'anyone/no one' under the negation, and شادمان is an adjective 'happy'. Together: 'no one in this offspring will remain happy.'
segments.s749-g211: سوی + ناسزایان = 'toward/into the hands of the revilers/insulters'. شود is the present/future passive/impersonal 'will become / will be', and تاج وتخت is تاج + و تخت ('crown and throne') with و written attached. The order is verb after indirect object, a poetic inversion: 'the crown and throne will go to the revilers.'
segments.s749-g212: تبه گردد = 'becomes ruined' (تبه = ruin/ill, گردد = becomes). این خسروانی درخت = 'this royal tree/house' (این = this, خسروانی = royal/Khosrowian, درخت used metaphorically as 'family-tree/lineage'). The verb folds back to describe the fate of the royal house.
segments.s749-g213: نماند 'will not remain' governs بزرگی 'greatness'; به introduces the beneficiary 'فرزند من' ('my son'). Word order is verb first (poetic), then the thing that will not remain and the person who would have it.
segments.s749-g214: نه ... نه/نه بر ... = 'nor ...' (here the initial نه is an adversative 'nor'); بر has the sense 'to/for' introducing additional beneficiaries. دوده here appears in a metaphorical sense of lineage/stock but does not match the provided literal senses (so marked -1). خوی (with the enclitic ش shown separately) means 'kin/relationship', پیوند 'bond/connection', and من 'my' attaches to the final item—together listing those who will lack greatness.
segments.s749-g215: همه دوستان ویژه = 'all (the) special/intimate friends'; شوند is the plural present/future 'will become', and دشمن is the predicate 'enemies'. Word order places the subject first, then predicate verb, then complement.
segments.s749-g216: بدین = ب + *دین/*این = 'by/through this' (here written as a split, meaning 'by this'); دوده = 'soot/defilement' (literal sense present), introducing the cause. بدگوی و بدتن شوند = 'they will become slanderers and ill-favored/ill-bodied' (بدگوی 'one who speaks ill', بدتن 'ill‑favored' or 'of bad body/character'), with شوند 'will become' as the verb ending the clause.
segments.s749-g41: مدارید is an imperative/jussive-form verb (2nd/3rd-person plural shape in classical diction) meaning 'deal with / endure / treat'; the verb appears before its object in this inverted, poetic order. کار جهان را is the definite direct object (کار = 'affair/work' with جهان attached as a genitive modifier, then را as the object marker). به رنج is a prepositional phrase of manner ('by/with hardship') indicating the means by which the action is performed.
segments.s749-g42: که introduces a subordinate clause giving the reason ('for/because'). از رنج is a source/agent phrase ('from/out of hardship'). یابد is a 3rd-person present/simple-form verb used generically ('one finds/attains'); it governs the predicate that follows. سرافراز گنج is the object/predicative noun phrase ('lofty/exalted treasure') with the adjective preceding the noun in classical order.
segments.s749-g43: دو داننده is a numeral + noun phrase ('two knowers/learned men'). بی‌کام is a postpositive adjective meaning 'unsuccessful/unfulfilled' describing those two. برخاستند is the past tense, 3rd-person plural of برخاستن ('they rose/stood up'), placed at the end as the finite predicate.
segments.s749-g44: پر از is a fixed expression 'full of' (پر functioning as adjective followed by the preposition از). آب مژگان literally 'water of the eyelashes' is a poetic kenning for tears (genitive construction). بیاراستند is the past 3rd-person-plural of بیاراستن 'to adorn/beautify' — the object (their faces/eyes/eyelashes) is implicit.
segments.s749-g45: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when/as'. خراد here functions as a proper name or epithet (classical diction uses nominal forms that can be names or nouns meaning 'wisdom/wise'); بر زین is the prepositional phrase 'on the saddle'. The coordinate و links the second name/word اشتاگشسپ (a corrupt/obscure form in the manuscript) — the clause implies both persons mounting.
segments.s749-g46: به فرمان is an adverbial phrase 'at the command/by the order' specifying circumstance. نشستند is the past 3rd-person-plural of نشستن 'they sat / they took their seats' (note: the printed lemma in the word list appears to be an editorial error; here it means 'sat/mounted' rather than 'wash'). هر دو 'both' emphasizes the two actors, and بر اسپ 'upon steeds/horse' indicates where they sat — the verb governs the whole action.
segments.s749-g47: بدیشان is the compound prepositional phrase 'to them' (ب + ایشان). چنین 'thus' frames what follows as reported speech. گفت is the past verb 'he said' introducing the reported clause; کز here functions as a subordinating conjunction 'that' (introducing the content of what was said). دل کنون ('the heart now' / 'from now in the heart') is an adverbial phrase marking immediacy or inner resolve ('now'/'from now').
segments.s749-g48: بباید is the modal auxiliary 'it is necessary / one must' (impersonal/modal necessity). گرفتن is the infinitive 'to take' and functions as the complement of the modal. ره طیسفون is the object: ره 'the road/way' with طیسفون (Ctesiphon) as the destination — the sequence modal + infinitive + object expresses obligation to set out for Ctesiphon.
segments.s749-g81: ب + دان (بِدان) is an imperative ‘know’ formed by prefixing ب- to دان. بد is a noun here ‘the bad/evil (deed)’, followed by the relative/complementizer که and کردی ‘you did’. بهانه منم is a verbless clause meaning ‘you made (me) a pretext’ (بهانه = ‘pretext/excuse’; منم = ‘I am’). The segment overall is an imperative admonition: ‘Know the evil that you did — you made me a pretext.’
segments.s749-g82: سخن را is a noun phrase with را as the definite/object marker (‘the speech/words’). نخست is an adjective/adverb ‘first’, آستانه is used metaphorically as ‘threshold’ or ‘preface’, and منم is the copula ‘I am’. Word order is topic (سخن را) + comment (نخست آستانه منم): ‘As for the speech, I am its first/preparatory threshold (i.e. its opening).’
segments.s749-g83: به یزدان is an oath ‘by God’ (به used in the sense ‘by’). که introduces the content clause. از من نبد is an archaic negative construction meaning ‘did not come from me / was not from me’ (نبد = was not). این گناه = ‘this sin’. Together: ‘By God, this sin was not from me.’
segments.s749-g84: نجستم is the past of جستن used here with a negative prefix (ن-), giving ‘I did not seek / I did not desire’. که introduces the subordinate clause of what was not desired: ویران شود گاه شاه = ‘that the king’s gāh (dwelling/time/place) become ruined’ (شود = ‘become/be’). So the whole means ‘I did not wish that the king’s abode/time be ruined.’
segments.s749-g85: کنون = ‘now’. پوزش = ‘apology/pardon’. این همه modifies بازجوی and means ‘all this / all these’; بازجوی here refers to interrogations/inquiries (the questioning). The noun phrase paces as ‘now, (offer) apology for all this questioning…’.
segments.s749-g86: بدین is the dative/formal directive ‘to these’ (here treated as be- + demonstrative), followed by نامداران ایران ‘the renowned/illustrious men of Iran’. بگوی is an imperative/ hortative ‘say / tell’ (i.e. ‘address / offer’). The whole segment directs the apology toward the named eminent men: ‘tell (your) apology to these renowned men of Iran.’
segments.s749-g87: ز هر بد که کردی = ‘from/for every bad (deed) that you did’ (ز in this poetic register = ‘for/with regard to’). به یزدان گرای is an exhortation ‘turn to God’ (به = ‘to’, یزدان = ‘God’; گرای is an imperative/verb ‘turn/face’ in archaic/poetic usage). The segment advises repentance: ‘For every wrong you have committed, turn to God.’
segments.s749-g88: کجا هست = rhetorical question ‘where is / who is there’. بر can mean ‘over/than’ or ‘besides’ in such comparative/rhetorical contexts. نیکوی رهنمای = ‘a good/virtuous guide’. Together this is a rhetorical tag: ‘where is there (any) better guide (than God)?’ — reinforcing the previous exhortation.
segments.s749-g201: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause here: 'when'. از دوده‌ام is a prepositional phrase ('from/out of my dūdah'—دوده with the 1st‑person suffix -ام meaning 'my'), expressing origin or prior obscurity. بخت روشن بگشت is a past‑tense clause in which بخت (subject) + روشن (predicate adjective) + بگشت (past verbal form 'turned/changed/returned' — an archaic/poetic past) means 'fortune became bright/turned bright.'
segments.s749-g202: غم آورد is a simple past clause where آورد (here 'brought' or intransitively 'came') is the verb and غم the subject/phenomenon ('sorrow appeared'). چون روشنایی گذشت is an adverbial time clause ('as/when the brightness passed') modifying غم آورد; گذشت is the past of گذریدن 'to pass.' Word order is typical: main verb (آورد) follows its subject, and the temporal clause follows or accompanies it.
segments.s749-g203: به اشتاد places the speech's addressee ('to Ashtād'). گفت is the speech verb 'said' (past tense, 3rd person), introducing direct speech; آنچ داری پیام is a nominal relative—آنچ 'that which/whatever' + داری 'you have' (present) + پیام 'message/tidings' — literally 'whatever message you have.' The clause is an instance of reported speech where the reporter verb precedes the quoted/object clause.
segments.s749-g204: ازان is a deictic 'from that/of that' pointing back to the referent of the inquiry. بی منش is an adjectival compound 'without character' (ill‑bred); کودک is the noun 'child' modified by زشت‌کام, where زشت ('vile') combines with کام ('desire') to yield 'of foul/indecent desire.' Together the phrase specifies the person about whom the speaker asks for news.
segments.s749-g205: وزان is a connective phrase ('and from that' / 'and of that'), linking to the preceding mention. بد سگالان is a descriptive noun phrase ('the bad Sagalān'), and که بی‌دانشند is a relative clause ('who are ignorant') where بی‌دانشند is a present‑tense predication 'they are without knowledge/ignorant.'
segments.s749-g206: ز بی دانشی is a prepositional/causal phrase ('from/because of ignorance') with ز (poetic form of از) + بی دانشی ('lack of knowledge'). ویژه is an adverb/qualifier meaning 'especially' or 'particularly.' بی رامش‌اند is a negated predicate: بی ('without') + رامش ('calm/peace') + اند (3pl copula) — 'they are without calm/they lack repose.' The clause states result/characteristic caused by ignorance.
segments.s749-g207: همان زان is an emphatic linker ('that very / from that'), pointing back to the foregoing; سپاه پراگندگان is a noun phrase where سپاه ('host, army') is qualified by پراگندگان ('scatterers' or 'those who scatter'), yielding 'the scattered host.' The head noun follows its modifiers in the usual Persian order.
segments.s749-g208: پر اندیشه is an adjectival phrase 'full of thought' (پر = 'full (of)'); و links the two adjectives; تیره‌دل is a compound adjective 'dark‑hearted / gloomy‑hearted.' بندگان is the head noun ('servants/people/subjects') modified by these adjectives. The whole phrase describes the composition and character of that scattered host.
segments.s749-g113: This is a nominal phrase introducing the agent: 'سخن گوی' functions as a compound 'speaker' (literally 'speech-sayer'), followed by the epithet/name 'خراد برزین'. 'نخست' (first) is an adverb placed after the name for emphasis and links forward to the verb in the next segment.
segments.s749-g114: 'زبان را' uses the object marker 'را' to mark 'زبان' (here = 'speech/utterance' rather than the physical tongue) as the direct object. 'به آب دلیری' is an instrumental/adverbial phrase introduced by 'به' (used here like 'with' / '-ly' in older Persian) meaning 'with the water of bravery'. 'بشست' is a 3rd-person singular past verb ('he washed'); the prefix 'ب-' is an older/perfective form common in epic Persian.
segments.s749-g115: Word order is inverted for poetic effect: 'گلینوش را' (Gelinush as the addressee, marked by object particle 'را') comes before the verb 'گفت' ('said/ addressed'), and the nominative agent 'فرخ قباد' follows the verb. In ordinary prose this would be 'فرخ قباد گلینوش را گفت' — 'Farrokh Qobad said to Gelinush.'
segments.s749-g116: 'به آرام' is an adverbial phrase ('in a calm/gentle manner') where 'به' functions adverbially ('-ly/by'). 'تاج کیان' is a possessive/construct phrase 'the crown of the Kianids/kings' (genitive relation implicit in poetry). 'برنهاد' is the past tense of برنهادن ('to place on, to raise upon'); here it is 3rd-person singular 'placed on/put on'.
segments.s749-g117: 'به ایران و توران و روم آگهیست' is a locative/ethical construction: 'به ... آگهیست' means 'it is known to/among ...'. The list 'ایران و توران و روم' names the realms where this is known. 'آگهیست' is a stative 3rd-person singular ('is known/it is reported').
segments.s749-g118: 'که' introduces the content clause ('that ...'). 'شیروی' is an epithet/name meaning 'lion-like, brave' and serves as the clause's subject. 'بر تخت' is the prepositional phrase 'on the throne'. 'شاهنشهیست' is a fused nominal predicate (شاهنشاهی + است) meaning 'is kingship' → idiomatically 'occupies the kingly throne' or 'is sovereign'.
segments.s749-g119: 'تواین' ('تو این') is an adverbial/locative compound meaning 'in this' or 'with this', introducing an enumerated list of items. The conjunction 'و' repeatedly connects the items: 'جوشن' (cuirass/armor), 'خود' (yourself/the person), 'گبر' (a Gabr — here used as a type/companion or pejorative epithet), and 'کمان' (bow). The whole phrase lists what the addressee brings or has.
segments.s749-g120: 'چه داری' is the interrogative 'what do you have/possess' (داری = 2nd-person present of داشتن). 'همی' is an older/periphrastic present/habitual marker (here emphasizing the present action/state). 'کیستت' is an archaic/clitic form meaning roughly 'who are you' (literally built from 'کیست' with a second-person clitic), and 'بد گمان' = 'of bad suspicion' (= 'I suspect you' / 'you are suspect'); together the final phrase expresses doubt/suspicion about the addressee's identity or intent.
segments.s749-g89: مگر introduces an exception/counterfactual (“if not / were it not that”). مر is an archaic short form meaning “me / to me” placed before the object for emphasis; تو را is the definite-object/oblique marker on “you.” او بود دستگیر is a past-copula clause (بود = was) with دستگیر used as a predicative noun meaning “helper, succourer.” Word order is emphatic/archaic: the helper (او) and the predicate (بود دستگیر) follow the marked objects.
segments.s749-g90: بدین here corresponds to classical بدین = به + این, i.e. “with/against these.” رنج‌هایی is the plural of رنج (hardships/sufferings). که introduces a relative clause (“which / that”). بودت is an older/poetic past-copular form meaning essentially “it was/was to you” (i.e. ‘served you as’), and گزیر is a noun meaning “refuge/way out/escape.” The whole segment says those hardships which served (as) your refuge/that you endured.
segments.s749-g91: دگر here means “again / moreover.” آنک is an archaic relative/particle meaning “that / which,” introducing the clause about the child. فرزند = “child/son.” بودت is a poetic past copula meaning roughly “was (to you)” (i.e. ‘your [son]’ or ‘you had’); دو هشت is a classical way of saying sixteen (literally “two eights”), a numerical expression placed after the predicate.
segments.s749-g92: شب و روز is the fixed pairing “night and day.” ایشان is the third-person plural pronoun “they/them” (here referring back to the two men/sons). به زندان گذشت is a light-verb construction where گذشت (past of گذر) means “passed / were spent” and به زندان indicates location “in/into prison”; together: “their nights and days passed in prison.”
segments.s749-g93: به در بر کسی is an idiomatic, slightly archaic phrase meaning “at anyone’s door / before anyone’s gate” (به + در + بر used together for emphasis). ایمن از تو means “safe from you” (ایمن = safe/secure, از تو = from you). نخفت is the past negative of خفت (“to sleep”), so the clause means “no one slept safely at anyone’s door because of you.” Note the negative assertion: اِیمن ... نخفت = did not sleep safe.
segments.s749-g94: ز (poetic form of از) + بیم تو = “from/because of fear of you.” بگذاشتندی is an archaic third-person-plural past form of گذاشتن (they put/they left/they hid), used here to mean they put aside/concealed; نهفت is a past participle/adjective from نهفتن meaning “hidden/concealed.” Together: “for fear of you they put (things) aside / concealed (them).”
segments.s749-g95: چو is an archaic conjunction meaning “when.” بشنید is the simple past “heard.” پیغام او = “his message” (پیغام = message; او = he/his). The verb precedes the subject here (typical in poetic inversion): “When they heard his message” with the subject rendered as این دو مرد (“these two men”).
segments.s749-g96: برفتند is the past plural of رفتن with the prefix بر- (they departed / went off). دل‌ها = hearts (plural). پر از = “full of” (پر used adjectivally with از). داغ here carries the poetic sense “burning grief/sorrow,” and درد means “pain/sorrow.” The clause describes their departing with hearts full of grief and pain.
segments.s749-g129: به کاری کجا آمدستی بگوی is a sequence of a prepositional phrase + interrogative + archaic past tense + imperative. به کاری ‘for what business/for what purpose’ uses به + کاری (کاری = ‘affair, task’ in context). کجا آمدستی is the interrogative ‘where did you come’ with آمدستی, an older 2nd-person singular past form. بگوی is an imperative (’tell!’ / ’say!’) addressing the listener to answer the preceding question.
segments.s749-g130: پس آنگه سخنهای من بازجوی strings temporal particles with the object and an imperative verb. پس آنگه ‘then/after that’ is emphatic sequencing. سخنهای من ‘my words’ (سخنها = speeches/words + plural suffix; من = possessive ‘my’). بازجوی here functions as a verbal imperative meaning ‘inquire/ask again’ (باز- ‘again’ + جوی ← جستن ‘seek/ask’), instructing the addressee to question or probe the speaker’s words.
segments.s749-g131: چنین داد پاسخ که فرخ قباد is an introductory reporting clause. چنین ‘thus’ modifies داد پاسخ ‘gave an answer/answered’ (داد = past 3rd-sg. ‘gave’, پاسخ = ‘answer’); که introduces the content of the answer. فرخ قباد follows as the person named in that answer (فرخ here an epithet attached to the name قباد).
segments.s749-g132: به خسرو مرا چند پیغام داد is the remainder of the reported clause with somewhat compact word order. به خسرو ‘to Khosrow’ (prepositional phrase marking the recipient of the messages). مرا را is the object pronoun + object marker (here written as م + را) indicating ‘to me’ / ‘me’ as indirect object — i.e. he entrusted me. چند پیغام ‘several/some messages’ (چند = some/several modifying پیغام). داد is past 3rd-sg. ‘gave’. The sense: ‘he gave me several messages for Khosrow’ or ‘entrusted me with several messages to deliver to Khosrow’.
segments.s749-g133: اگر باز خواهی بگویم همه is a conditional clause plus the speaker’s reply. اگر ‘if’. باز ‘again/further’ here means ‘if you still/want more’. خواهی is 2nd-person present/future of خواستن ‘to want’ (’you want/will want’), so together = ‘if you wish’. بگویم is 1st-person-singular present/subjunctive of گفتن used prospectively ‘I will tell / let me tell’; in classical Persian bگویم after اگر expresses the speaker’s offer. همه ‘all’ is the object placed after the verb (’all [of them]’).
segments.s749-g134: پیام جهاندار شاه رمه is a nominal phrase giving what will be told: پیام ‘message/tidings’. جهاندار ‘world-holder’, an epithet meaning sovereign/conqueror, and شاه ‘king’ form a stacked title (epithet + title). رمه ‘flock/throng’ here is used poetically with شاه (شاهِ رمه = ‘king of the host/multitude’). Together: ‘the messages of the world-ruling king, king of the host.’
segments.s749-g135: گلینوش گفت این گرانمایه مرد is a simple reporting clause. گلینوش (proper name) is the speaker, گفت is past 3rd-sg. ‘said’. این گرانمایه مرد is a following noun phrase ‘this esteemed/precious man’ (این = this; گرانمایه = precious/esteemed; مرد = man/person/hero). Word order is: speaker + said + the quoted/introduced NP.
segments.s749-g136: که داند سخنها همه یاد کرد is a relative clause modifying ‘man’. که = ‘who/that’. داند is the present/old-form of دانستن ‘knows’; in classical epic it is often used where English would use past (‘who knew/was able’). سخنها همه ‘all the words/speeches’ (سخنها = words/speeches, همه = all). یاد کرد is a light-verb construction: یاد ‘remembrance’ + کرد ‘did/made’ = ‘he remembered/recited/recounted’. Together: ‘who knew/remembered all the words.’
segments.s749-g97: برین گونه is the frozen phrase بر + این + گونه = "in this manner/thus." تا here means "as far as" and introduces the extent (کشور طیسفون = the realm of Ctesiphon). Word order is poetic: the demonstrative compound (برین گونه) precedes the clause of extent.
segments.s749-g98: همه governs both noun phrases; دیده پرآب is a nominal phrase meaning "eyes full of water (tears)" (پرآب = "full of water/teary"). دل پر ز خون uses the pattern "پر + ز + N" = "full of N"; ز here is the archaic/poetic preposition "of/from." The sentence omits an explicit copula, typical of poetic Persian.
segments.s749-g99: نشسته is a participle/adjectival form "seated/sitting." بدر (the full moon) is the subject, بر گلینوش is a prepositional phrase "on Golīnūsh," and بود is the past copula—literal order: "(the) full moon was sitting on Golīnūsh." This is a poetic, stative description.
segments.s749-g100: که introduces a subordinate/simile clause ("that/so that"). گفتی here functions idiomatically like "one might say" or "it seemed," not as a literal modern conversational noun; the provided sense list doesn't include this use (see senses mapping). زمین is "the earth; the ground." ز + و is the poetic contraction of ز + او ("from/of it"), so ز marks the source/agent of the seething. پر از جوش = "full of boiling/surge" (جوش = seething/boiling). بود is the past copula. Overall: "that one would say the earth was full of seething from it."
segments.s749-g101: همه لشکرش = "all his army/host," with the possessive suffix on لشکر. یک سر آراسته is an idiomatic description: literally "one head adorned," used here to mean "completely/adorned throughout" (i.e., each man was fully dressed/armoured). The predicate copula is omitted (typical poetic ellipsis).
segments.s749-g102: کشیده is the past participle/verb "drawn/pulled" (here: "had drawn" as in drawing swords). همه qualifies the following noun تیغ (swords). و links to پیراسته, another adjectival/past-participial description: "and adorned/arrayed." The clause describes simultaneous actions/states: they had drawn swords and were arrayed.
segments.s749-g103: ابا here is archaic/uncertain in this context (the supplied senses do not match the usage in the line); جوشن = "mail/armour" and و = "and." خود بسته is a reflexive participial phrase: خود (self) + بسته (bound/girt) = "having girded himself"; بسته is a past participle. میان here functions adverbially as "in the middle" (i.e., about the waist/midsection). Together: "with cuirass and girded about the middle."
segments.s749-g104: همان = "the same/that very," تازی qualifies اسپان (Arab / Arabian), and اسپان is a plural/collective for horses or cavalry (poetic for cavalry). به marks position or direction; برگستوان is an archaic compound (here best understood as "on the shoulder/shoulders") — the phrase therefore describes the Arab cavalry with lances on their shoulders. The supplied lemma's single sense for برگستوان did not match this locative meaning, so that item is uncertain in the senses map.
segments.s749-g153: Word order: verb + indirect object + vocative + (proper) name. بدو is the prepositional phrase 'to him' (بِ + او contracted), گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (here the lexical sense list lacked the verb sense), شاها is the vocative 'O king', and انوشه بدی is a personal name placed after the verb (a common poetic/order-flexible placement of subject). Together they introduce reported speech.
segments.s749-g154: مبادا is a formulaic jussive/optative marker 'may it not be / lest'; دل تو is the possessed noun phrase 'your heart'; نژند is an adjective 'base, debased' (here meaning 'made base/tainted'); از بدی = 'by/from evil' (از marking source/cause). The whole clause is a wish/prayer: 'May your heart not become base because of evil.'
segments.s749-g155: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. اشتاد و خراد برزین is a coordinated subject phrase; here خراد functions as an epithet 'wise' modifying برزین (Barzin), so the phrase reads 'Ashtad and the wise Barzin'. به شاه is a prepositional phrase 'to the king' marking their destination. The finite verb is deferred to the next segment.
segments.s749-g156: پیام is the direct object 'message/tidings', آوریدند is the past plural 'they brought' (آوردن + -ند plural), زان = 'from that' (زان = ز + آن), and بارگاه 'that court/royal hall' is the source. Together: 'they brought a message from that court.'
segments.s749-g157: بخندید is a past-tense verb 'he laughed'; خسرو immediately follows as the subject (typical poetic word order). به آواز is an adverbial phrase 'aloud / in a voice'. گفت is the reporting verb 'said' (the provided senses did not include the verb sense). The segment sets up reported speech that follows.
segments.s749-g158: که introduces the content clause of 'said' ('that' / 'that...'). این رای تو = 'this counsel/opinion of yours' (رای here = counsel/plan). با خرد juxaposes 'with wisdom' (با + خرد), نیست = 'is not', and جفت literally 'pair' is used idiomatically: 'not a match' or 'not in accord'. The clause therefore means 'that this counsel of yours is not in accordance with wisdom.'
segments.s749-g159: گرو here is an archaic contracted conditional (گر او = 'if he') — the provided word senses did not include this contraction, so no direct match. شهریارست is a predicative construction 'is king' (شهریار + -است fused as -ست), پس = 'then', and من کیم is an inverted copular interrogative 'who am I?' The line is rhetorical: 'If he is king, then who am I?'
segments.s749-g160: درین is the fused prepositional phrase 'در + این' = 'in this'; تنگ زندان = 'narrow prison' (تنگ = narrow/close, زندان = prison); ز بهر = 'for the sake of / for' (ز marking cause/reason + بهر 'for'); چیم is a colloquial/poetic interrogative 'what of me?' or 'what am I here for?' Together: 'in this narrow prison, for what am I (here)?' i.e. 'Why am I in this narrow prison?'
segments.s749-g177: بهی تناور گرفته بدست is a compact, somewhat poetic clause: بهی (an honorific epithet) + تناور (adjective 'tall/robust') form a noun phrase 'the towering Behi'. گرفته is a past participle 'taken/seized' and بدست ('in/by hand') completes the verbal phrase 'گرفته بدست' = 'taken by the hand' (i.e. 'took by the hand' or 'grasped'). Word order is inverted from modern prose for poetic rhythm.
segments.s749-g178: دژم خفته بر جایگاه نشست is the main clause: دژم خفته is a nominal phrase 'the grim/sullen one who had been sleeping' (دژم = 'grim/sullen', خفته = 'sleeping/asleep'). بر جایگاه is the locative prepositional phrase 'upon the seat/throne' and نشست is the past-tense verb 'sat'. The verb is simple past; the nominal phrase serves as the subject that performs the action of sitting.
segments.s749-g179: چودید is the fused poetic form of چو + دید ('when he saw'); it introduces a temporal clause. آن دو مرد گرانمایه را is the definite object phrase 'those two esteemed men' with را marking them as the definite object of دید. Word order places the temporal marker before the object for emphasis.
segments.s749-g180: به دانایی اندر سرمایه را is an evaluative phrase: به here marks the regard ('as' / 'to be'), دانایی = 'wisdom', اندر سرمایه = 'in (their) capital/treasure' (poetic expression meaning 'a source or treasure'), and the final را again marks the phrase as object/target of his judgment. Literally 'as a capital in wisdom' → 'as a treasure/source of wisdom.'
segments.s749-g181: از آن خفتگی خویشتن کرد راست is a reflexive motion clause: از آن خفتگی = 'from that drowsiness', خویشتن کرد = 'made himself' / 'roused himself' (کرد used causatively/past), and راست = 'straight'/'upright' (i.e., 'straightened up' or 'sat up erect'). The structure is 'از X خویشتن کرد راست' = 'he roused himself from X and sat up.'
segments.s749-g182: جهان آفریننده را یار خواست is a straightforward transitive clause: جهان آفریننده ('the world-creator' = the Creator/God) is marked by را as the object, یار = 'companion/ally/friend', and خواست is the past-tense verb 'sought/wanted'. The sense is 'he sought the Creator as a companion.'
segments.s749-g183: به بالین نهاد آن گرامی بهی is a locative action: به بالین ('to/at the bedside' — بالین here = bedside/pillow) + نهاد (past of نهادن, 'placed/put/laid himself') and آن گرامی بهی is the nominative noun phrase 'that honored Behi'. Poetic ordering places the locative phrase before the verb.
segments.s749-g184: بدان تا بپرسید ز هر دو رهی expresses purpose: بدان (an archaic fused form 'به + آن' = 'to that end' / 'for that') + تا ('so that/in order that') + بپرسید (past-tense poetic form 'he asked' or purpose-subjunctive 'he might ask') and ز هر دو رهی = 'from each of the two wayfarers'. Together: 'to that end, so that he would ask of each of the two wayfarers.'
segments.s749-g17: ز introduces a causal phrase here ('because/of'), بایسته‌تر کار is a noun phrase 'the more necessary task/affair' (بایسته with comparative -تر). پیشی مرا = 'precedence to me' where مرا is the clitic sequence (م + را) marking the person/object ('me') and the definite/object marker; word order is flexible in classical Persian, so the whole phrase reads 'because the more necessary affair was my precedence.'
segments.s749-g18: که introduces a subordinate clause giving the reason ('for / since'). افزون بود is a copular clause with افزون 'greater/more' and بود (past/copula) 'was'. فر و خویشی مرا = 'my farr and kinship' (فر 'royal glory', خویشی 'kinship'); مرا again marks possession/object. The clause therefore says 'for my glory and kinship were greater.'
segments.s749-g19: پیامی فرستم is a first-person verb phrase 'I will/send a message' (فرستم here expresses the speaker's intention). به نزد پدر = 'to the presence of / to my father' (به نزد = toward/in the presence of). The whole segment means 'I will send a message to my father.'
segments.s749-g20: بگویم is first-person subjunctive/future 'I will say / I shall tell'. بدو is the dative clitic 'to him'. این سخن is the direct object 'these words / this speech'. در به در is an adverbial idiom literally 'door to door' (در = 'door' here) meaning 'going from door to door' or 'wandering/going about'. Word order places the verb before its objects, a common classical Persian pattern.
segments.s749-g21: ز introduces cause 'because of'. ناخوب کاری is a compound noun 'an ill/bad deed' (ناخوب = bad, کاری = deed/act). که او راندست is a relative clause 'that he rādast/ran-dast' — راندست is a past verbal form meaning he did/caused/drove (here 'committed' or 'perpetrated'). Together: 'because of the bad deed that he committed.'
segments.s749-g22: برین گونه کاری = بر این گونه کارِی, 'upon/with this kind of act' (بر = 'upon/over', ین = orthographic variant of این, گونه = 'kind', کاری = 'act'). به پیش آمدست is a verbal phrase 'has come to pass / has happened' (آمدست = 'has come'). Altogether: 'such an act has come to pass.' Note the archaic copular/perfective form آمدست.
segments.s749-g23: به یزدان کند پوزش او از گناه is structured as an optative/wish: 'کند' is used in the sense 'may he do' (subjunctive/imperative optative). به یزدان = 'to God', پوزش = 'apology/pardon', او = 'he', از گناه = 'for/from the sin' — so 'may he ask pardon of God for his sin.'
segments.s749-g24: گراینده گردد uses گراینده (a present‑participle/adjectival form 'turning/one who inclines') with گردد (subjunctive/optative 'may become' or 'may be turned') to express 'may he become/turn'. به آیین و راه = 'to the ritual/custom and the way/path'. So the clause prays that he be inclined toward proper ritual and the right path.
segments.s749-g145: پیامیست is an archaic/poetic copular phrase (پیام + است) meaning 'it is a message'. کان introduces a relative clause ('that' / 'which') here. تیغ بار آورد is the relative clause: تیغ = 'sword', بار = 'load/burden', and آورد is 3rd-person past 'brought' or 'bore', so together 'that bore/ brought a sword as its burden.'
segments.s749-g146: سر سرکشان is a genitive noun phrase: سر 'head' + سرکشان 'the rebellious ones' (an older plural form), i.e. 'the heads of the rebels.' در کنار آورد is a verbal phrase with در کنار 'to/into the side/nearby' + آورد (past 3sg) 'brought', so 'brought the rebels' heads to the side/near him.'
segments.s749-g147: The phrase is a nominal sentence with omitted copula: تو اکنون 'you now' + ز خسرو 'from Khosrow' (source/agent) + برین بارخواه 'the foremost petitioner'. برین is formed from بر + -ین (an adjectival/enhancing element) meaning 'supreme/foremost', and بارخواه is a noun 'seeker/petitioner'.
segments.s749-g148: بدان is an imperative formed from ب + دان (here ب is the prefix used in older imperatives, دان = 'know') meaning 'know this.' تا introduces a purpose clause 'so that' and governs the following verb بگویم (first-person present/subjunctive 'that I may say/tell'). پیامش is a possessed noun 'his message' (پیام + ش = his), and ز شاه 'from the king' locates the source of that message.
segments.s749-g149: گلینوش is a proper name (subject). بشنید is past 3rd-person 'she heard.' و coordinates the next action. بر پای جست is an idiomatic past action: بر + پای 'upon (her) feet' + جست (past 3sg of 'to leap') = 'sprang to her feet.'
segments.s749-g150: همه بندها رابهم برشکست is compacted: همه بندها 'all the bonds/fetters' + را به هم (here written رابهم) is the object-marker را plus به هم 'together' and برشکست is past 'broke/shattered.' So the clause means 'she broke all the bonds together' i.e. 'she broke all the fetters.'
segments.s749-g151: بر شاه شد is a verbal phrase meaning 'he turned/ directed (it) toward the king' or 'he set himself against the king' (بر + شاه + شد, past 3sg). دست کرده is a participial phrase 'having made/raised (his) hand' (دست + کرده), and بکش is an imperative which can mean 'draw/strike' or 'kill!' depending on context; here it functions as an order/exclamation following the preparatory participle.
segments.s749-g152: چنان چون is a correlative ('in such a manner as'), followed by بباید (present-obligative/modal 'ought to / must') expressing what should be done. پرستارفش is an older/compound form functioning as the logical object/predicate of بباید ('as his protector/guardian ought [to do]'). The exact lexical composition of پرستارفش is archaic, so this word is treated as a single (context-dependent) unit here.
segments.s749-g161: که introduces a subordinate / reported-clause ('that'). از من is a prepositional phrase 'from me'. همی is an archaic continuous/intensifying particle placed before the predicate (here marking ongoing or emphatic aspect). بار here means 'occasion'/'time' ("this time / this matter") and combines with بایدت + خواست: بایدت is the modal 'it is necessary for you' (باید + enclitic -ت 'for you'), and خواست is the verbal noun / noun 'asking/request' used as the complement of بایدت to yield 'you must ask (this)'.
segments.s749-g162: This is a pair of conditional clauses introduced by اگر 'if'. کژ is an adjectival/adverbial word meaning 'crooked / wrong' and modifies گویی; گویی here is the verb 'you say / you speak' (present), so اگر کژ گویی = 'if you speak crookedly / falsely'. The second اگر introduces a parallel alternative: اگر راه راست literally 'if the way is straight' (elliptical for 'if you speak the straight way' or 'if the matter is straight/truthful').
segments.s749-g163: بیامد is a past tense verb 'came' (VSO order is common in classical Persian: verb before subject). گلینوش is the proper name (subject). نزد is a preposition meaning 'in the presence of / to' and governs the following noun; گوان here follows نزد and marks the people/party she approached (the exact semantic label of گوان is obscure in the provided senses, so it is treated as the target audience).
segments.s749-g164: بگفت is a past-tense/reporting verb 'she said' (بـ- prefix + گفت). این سخن is 'this speech/these words' (این = 'this', سخن = 'speech'). گفتن is the verbal noun/infinitive 'the saying' functioning to mark that the speech was an utterance, and پهلوان follows as the agent/possessor: 'the saying of the hero' = 'spoken by the hero.' segments.s749-g166: بگویید is an imperative (2nd-person plural) 'speak / say / ask' directing the addressees to address the prisoner. و is the conjunction 'and'. گفتار او is the direct object 'his speech/what he has to say' (گفتار = speech, او = his/he). بشنوید is another 2nd-person plural imperative 'hear / listen' forming the sequence 'speak and listen to his words.'
segments.s749-g167: دو مرد is a numeral + noun 'two men'. خردمند is an adjective 'wise' modifying مرد. و links the adjectives. پاکیزه‌گوی is a compound adjective 'pure-/clean-speaking' or 'truthful-speaking' describing the men (in Persian compounds like this follow the noun).
segments.s749-g168: به here means 'with / using' (marks the instrument). دستار is 'turban' (the cloth). چینی qualifies دستار ('Chinese' — either style or material). بپوشید is a past or narrative form 'they put on / they covered' (بـ- prefix + پوشید), and روی here means 'upon the face' (they covered their faces with the turbans). segments.s749-g193: بهی is used as a noun meaning 'goodness/that which is good' and appears before the verb as its object. رانهادند is a 3rd-person plural past-form (archaic/persianized) meaning roughly 'they caused/put/let' (here 'bestowed/placed'); بر + شادورد is a prepositional phrase 'upon Shadvard' with بر governing the following name.
segments.s749-g194: همی‌بود is an imperfective/past recurrent auxiliary phrase meaning 'was / continued to be' (here 'stood'). برپای is a verblike adverbial phrase 'on foot / standing upright'. پیش این دو مرد is a locative phrase 'before/in the presence of these two men' with the determiner این modifying مرد.
segments.s749-g195: پر here is a preposed modifier meaning 'full (of)' and combines with اندیشه ('thought') to make 'filled with thought.' شد is the past copulative/verb 'became/was made,' and نامدار is the subject 'the famed/renowned one.' از + بهی marks the cause/referent 'because of / at the sight of the good.'
segments.s749-g196: ندید is the past negative verb 'did not see.' اندر is a locative/prepositional adverb 'within/in.' و links the clauses, and هیچ فال بهی means 'no/none (هیچ) omen (فال) of good (بهی),' with بهی used substantively ('good/fortunate sign').
segments.s749-g197: همانگه is an adverb meaning 'at that moment.' سوی آسمان is a directional prepositional phrase 'toward the sky/heaven.' کرد + روی is a verbal phrase 'made (his) face' i.e. 'turned his face' — the verb کرد combines with the noun روی to express the action of turning the face.
segments.s749-g198: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' qualifying گفت, which here functions as the past verb 'he said' (note: the listed lemma senses do not include the verbal 'to say' exactly). کای is a vocative 'O,' addressing داور, 'the Judge' (here a divine/judicial epithet). راست گوی is a descriptive compound 'right/truth-speaking' (راست + گوی).
segments.s749-g199: The initial که is a relative/subordinating particle 'that/which.' برگیرد is a 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive/optative form 'may take up / will take up.' آن را که is the fused relative 'that which/what,' with تو افگنی a subordinate clause where تو is 'you' and افگنی is the archaic 2nd-person form 'you cast/throw' (an older verbal ending -ی).
segments.s749-g200: Again که begins the clause 'that...'; پیوندد here functions as a verb 'may join / bind together' (the lemma entries list related nouns; context makes clear the verbal sense). آن را is the object with the enclitic را for definiteness/emphasis, and که تو بشکنی is the relative clause 'that which you break,' with بشکنی an archaic 2nd-person form (verb 'to break').
segments.s749-g49: پیامی (object) precedes the verb and is indefinite by the -ی ending; رسانید is a past tense (simple/historical) 3rd‑person singular verb meaning 'delivered/ conveyed.' نزد پدر is a prepositional phrase ('to/into the presence of the father'); نزد here marks direction/recipient rather than physical proximity. segments.s749-g50: This is a nominal phrase giving the content of the message rather than a full clause: سخن (speech/news) + یادگیری (of learning/study) forms a noun phrase 'news of learning.' همه is a postmodifier 'all' qualifying what is reported, and در بدر is a locative phrase 'in Badr' (بدر here is a place-name). Classical Persian often omits the ezāfe linking these nouns; their relationship is understood by juxtaposition.
segments.s749-g51: بگویی here functions as a hortatory/optative or reported‑speech verb ('one might say' / 'you could say'); in classical Persian this form introduces a reported or hypothetical remark. که introduces the content clause. segments.s749-g52: نه marks a coordinate negation 'nor'; ایرانیان را is topicalized (را after ایرانیان marks them as the topic/affected group). بد is the predicate adjective 'bad' and این دستگاه is the noun phrase 'this device/system/establishment' (دستگاه = court/arrangement). segments.s749-g53: که introduces a purpose/wish clause. باد here is the optative particle 'may' (classical usage) rather than the common noun 'wind.' افـره ایزدی is the object or appellation modified by ایزدی ('divine'); the noun appears without an ezāfe in this poetic register. یافتی is the past form of یافتن used after the optative باد to express a wish: 'may you find/obtain.'
segments.s749-g54: چو = 'when/as.' از + نیکوی روی is a poetic locative/causal phrase 'by/from your good/fair face' (نیکوی is an adjectival form modifying روی). بر تافتی contains the verb تافتی (from تافتن) with the preposition بر indicating direction/target; تافتن here carries the sense 'shine/turn toward' so the clause means 'when your fair face turns/shines toward (it).' segments.s749-g55: یکی آنک is a set phrase 'one who' / 'one such that' (یکی + relative آنک). ناباک is an adjective 'innocent/undefiled' describing the kind of person referred to; خون پدر ('the father's blood') is the patient/topic of the following verb. segments.s749-g56: نریزد is the negative form of ریختن (here classical/poetic present/contrary-to-fact sense 'does not shed' / 'would not spill'); in older Persian forms like نریزد are used where modern Persian would have نمی‌ریزد or نریزد as a jussive. ز (poetic form of از) marks source/agent ('from/at'), تن پاک زاده پسر is a compound noun phrase 'the son born of a pure body' (تن = body, پاک = pure, زاده = born/of lineage, پسر = son). segments.s749-g185: The clause begins with the proper name 'بهی' (subject). 'زان دو بالش' is a poetic prepositional phrase ('زان' = 'from those') indicating source ('from those two pillows'). segments.s749-g186: 'بی‌آزار' is an adjectival/adverbial phrase meaning 'harmless/inoffensive' describing how the action was done. segments.s749-g187: 'بدین گونه' is a fixed expression meaning 'in this manner/thus' (here segmented as 'ب' + 'دین' + 'گونه' in the data; functionally 'بدین گونه' = 'in this way'). segments.s749-g188: 'همی‌گشت' is an imperfective/habitual-form with the archaic prefix 'همی-' meaning 'kept on turning/going about'. segments.s749-g189: 'به پویید' is an older/periphrastic past meaning 'he hurried/advanced' (پوییدن = to advance/seek), with the prefixed 'به' giving motion or sequence. 'اشتاد' here is a proper name. segments.s749-g190: 'به مالیدش' combines the past of مالیدن 'to rub/smear' with the clitic object '-ش' = 'him' ('he rubbed him' or 'anointed him'). segments.s749-g191: 'جهاندار' is the subject meaning 'the sovereign/king'. 'از اشتاد' uses the preposition 'از' ('from') to indicate the point/person he turns away from (Ishtad). segments.s749-g192: 'بدان' is a compacted locative/directional phrase (بـ + آن) meaning 'toward him/that'; 'تا' introduces the purpose/result clause 'so that'.
